methodology_config.name: agile
methodology_config.global_instructions:
You are generating artifacts for a flexible Agile project. Agile emphasizes iterative delivery, continuous feedback, and adaptive planning. This is general Agile — not strictly Scrum or Kanban, but borrows from both.
Rules: - Focus on delivering working software in short iterations - Requirements should be lightweight and adaptable — not over-specified - Prioritize collaboration and feedback over comprehensive documentation - Every artifact should be useful to a developer starting work tomorrow - Use plain language — avoid excessive formality - Group work into manageable iterations that deliver value incrementally

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Extract modules as feature areas that can be developed and delivered independently. Each module should represent a vertical slice of functionality that delivers end-user value.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: - Use User Story format where natural: "As a [role], I want [feature], so that [benefit]." - Keep requirements lightweight — enough detail to start development, not a full specification - Include acceptance criteria that are clear and testable - Prioritize: Must Have, Should Have, Nice to Have - Each requirement should be deliverable within 1-2 iterations
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: - Frame as quality attributes with measurable targets - Identify which NFRs are "must have for launch" vs "improve over time" - Keep practical — avoid over-engineering targets that aren't grounded in the SOW
artifact_overrides.task: - Break work into tasks completable by one developer in 1-3 days - Include effort estimates in hours - Group tasks by iteration or feature area - Focus on what to build, not what to plan or document - Link every task to its parent requirement
artifact_overrides.test_case: - Write test cases that validate acceptance criteria directly - Include both happy path and key edge cases - Keep steps concise and actionable — avoid over-detailed procedures - Prioritize: test the most valuable and risky features first - Include exploratory testing suggestions for complex features
artifact_overrides.architecture: - Design for iterative delivery — components should support incremental deployment - Prefer simple architectures that can evolve over upfront over-engineering - Identify the minimum viable architecture needed for the first iteration - Highlight decisions that should be deferred vs decisions needed now
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: - Focus on risks to iterative delivery: unclear requirements, scope creep, technical unknowns - Include risks around team communication and stakeholder availability - Mitigation plans should be lightweight and actionable - Identify risks that need resolution before the first iteration starts
